Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

- World model: All symbols (child objects) are black (B). The upper grid has 6 sky blue + 6 pink tiles with black symbols. Lower bars have 5 sky blue (static) + 5 pink (editable) tiles with black symbols. UP/DOWN cycle the pink symbol at the cursor position through 7 states. LEFT/RIGHT move the cursor. - Plan: Get full hashes for all relevant objects, then try to match pink cycle states to sky blue bar symbols or upper grid symbols.

end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Each position likely has its own cycle (different from position 0's cycle). Initial pink bar symbols don't all match upper grid symbols. Need to determine the goal - possibly matching pink bar to upper pink tiles or sky blue bar.
Recent findings: Initial pink bar has 5 symbols, only 1 matches an upper grid symbol. The cycle at position 0 has 7 states, but initial pink bar symbols at positions 1 and 2 are not in the position-0 cycle, suggesting position-dependent cycles.
Plan: Re-measure the cycle at position 0 with full hashes to verify, then check if cycles differ by position. Then try to match pink bar to upper pink tiles.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, ensure cursor is at position 0
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
white_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
cursor_col = white_nodes[0]['boundary'][0][1]
pos_map = {15: 0, 22: 1, 29: 2, 36: 3, 43: 4}
cursor_pos = pos_map.get(cursor_col, -1)
print(f"Cursor at col {cursor_col}, position {cursor_pos}")

# Move to position 0 if needed
if cursor_pos != 0:
    steps = (0 - cursor_pos) % 5
    action(['RIGHT'] * steps)

# Now measure the full cycle at position 0
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
current_hash = nodes[cid]['hash']
current_px = nodes[cid]['pixels']

print(f"\nStarting at pos 0: hash={current_hash}, px={current_px}")

# Cycle through all 7 states and record full hashes
cycle_states = []
for i in range(7):
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    nodes = seg['nodes']
    p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
    cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
    h = nodes[cid]['hash']
    px = nodes[cid]['pixels']
    cycle_states.append((h, px))
    action(['UP'])

print("\nCycle at position 0 (full hashes):")
for i, (h, px) in enumerate(cycle_states):
    print(f"  State {i}: {h} ({px}px)")

# Also check: what is the current state at position 0 after cycling?
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
print(f"\nAfter 7 UPs, pos 0 hash={nodes[cid]['hash']}, px={nodes[cid]['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

    action(['RIGHT'] * steps)

# Now measure the full cycle at position 0
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
current_hash = nodes[cid]['hash']
current_px = nodes[cid]['pixels']

print(f"\nStarting at pos 0: hash={current_hash}, px={current_px}")

# Cycle through all 7 states and record full hashes
cycle_states = []
for i in range(7):
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    nodes = seg['nodes']
    p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
    cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
    h = nodes[cid]['hash']
    px = nodes[cid]['pixels']
    cycle_states.append((h, px))
    action(['UP'])

print("\nCycle at position 0 (full hashes):")
for i, (h, px) in enumerate(cycle_states):
    print(f"  State {i}: {h} ({px}px)")

# Also check: what is the current state at position 0 after cycling?
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
p_tiles = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'P' and n['boundary'][0][0] > 35]
cid = p_tiles[0]['children'][0]
print(f"\nAfter 7 UPs, pos 0 hash={nodes[cid]['hash']}, px={nodes[cid]['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
